calendar.matters:
Petition To Determine Ownership Of Property, Damages, Double Damages, Compel An Accounting, Breach Of Contract, Declaratory Relief, Preliminary And Pe
Off Calendar Oct-05-2021 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Property, Damages, Double Damages, Compel An Accounting, Breach Of Contract, Declaratory Relief, Preliminary And Pe
Off Calendar Aug-17-2021 Continued To Oct-05-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Cain appeared by zoom for Grace Plimpton.
Franchesca Callejo and Vincent Yu appeared by zoom for and with Adrianne Vincent by zoom.

Ms. Cain stated that they had a temporary Trustee appointed yesterday in Contra Costa, that she received an executed document from Adrianne Vincent to pay the settlement funds and that based on the settlement agreement, she is required to file a dismissal when payment is received. The Petition to Determine Ownership of Property, etc. is continued to October 5, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Property, Damages, Double Damages, Compel An Accounting, Breach Of Contract, Declaratory Relief, Preliminary And Pe
Off Calendar Jul-13-2021 Continued To Aug-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Cain, via ZOOM, for Grace Plimpton, Petitioner; Franchesca Callejo, and Vincent Yu, via ZOOM, for Adrienne Vincent, via ZOOM, Objector; Daniel Bernhard, via ZOOM, for Shelby Moore III, Objector.

The court requests an update regarding the settlement agreement and mutual release approved by Contra Costa County court. Ms. Cain reports that she could file a request for dismissal on behalf of her client but pursuant to the terms of the settlement, she cannot do so until her client receives payment. Mr. Bernhard advises that his client is ready to make a distribution but that he is waiting for information on where to make the payment. Both Mr. Yu and Ms. Callejo offer oral testimony regarding the terms of the settlement. Ms. Cain then suggests an appointment of a professional fiduciary to serve as Trustee of the settlement. After additional discussion, the court advises counsel that if there is no resolution by the next calling of this matter, the matter will be set for trial and possible case management dates.

The Petition to Determine Ownership of Property, etc., is continued to August 17, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Property, Damages, Double Damages, Compel An Accounting, Breach Of Contract, Declaratory Relief, Preliminary And Pe
Off Calendar Jun-08-2021 Continued To Jul-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Property, Damages, Double Damages, Compel An Accounting, Breach Of Contract, Declaratory Relief, Preliminary And Pe
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Continued To Jun-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Bernhard, via ZOOM, counsel for Selby Moore III, Trustee; Vincent Yu, via ZOOM, Franchesca Callejo, via ZOOM, Co-Counsel for Adrianne Vincent, Interested Party; Carolyn Cain, via ZOOM, counsel for Grace Plimpton, FKA Grace Polly Vincent, Petitioner in related case.

At the outset, the court confirms with counsel that a mediation is scheduled for the beginning of May 2021 in the San Francisco and Contra Costa County matters. Mr. Bernhard confirms that the Temporary Restraining Order ("TRO") filed in the Contra Costa matter was vacated by that court in early 2021. However, thereafter, Grace M. Plimpton, filed a new application in the Contra Costa action seeking a new TRO. That application for a TRO has been continued until after the parties have completed the mediation set for May, 2021. Ms. Cain advises that a hearing on a preliminary injunction is currently set for the Contra Costa matter and asks this court to do the same. The court advises counsel that any discussions regarding a preliminary injunction in this matter will not be heard without a submission of a formal motion of which at this time, there is none. Regardless, the judge in the Contra Costa matter would like the parties to mediate prior to ruling on the petition for a preliminary injunction to which this court is in agreement with. The court then questions as to who is the owner of the property located at 160 Sea Cliff. Mr. Berhard reports that the property is included in a Irrevocable Trust dated 2012. Mr. Bernard also reports that the property is currently vacant and is being worked on to place on the market for sale. After further discussion, the court requests Ms. Cain to prepare, file and serve on all parties a statement regarding the status of mediation in this matter by no later that June 01,2021, one week prior to the next hearing date.

The Petition to Determine Ownership of Property, etc., was continued to June 08, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Not Reported.
